description: This long-sleeved, half-button-down upper garment (barong) is embroidered, made from piña (pineapple) and cotton fabric. The cotton collar and cuffs feature embroidery including flowers, leaves, rope-like curvilinear lines forming geometric designs, and scallops along the edges. Similar embroidery decorates the chest area around the buttons. The body, mainly piña fabric, features cotton-thread embroidery that creates diamond shapes with a floral design at the center of each. The cotton threads, called Spanish trensillas, are attached to the piña fabric using a couching stitch. Two slits at the front hem provide ease for the abdomen.
condition: Poor but stable condition. All chest and cuff buttons are missing; a large hole at the nape has been restored with fine net cloth; a few tiny holes, stains, and loose threads; apparent discoloration on the collar and cuffs.

images.alt: Cream piña barong with long sleeves, a diamond lattice of fine embroidered sprigs, and an embroidered floral placket down the button front
Close-up of the barong's collar and embroidered placket, its openwork floral and geometric stitching visible through the sheer piña fabric

rights: Images courtesy of the Ethnologisches Museum, Staatliche Museen zu Berlin.
